questions.q: Who was the asura king father of Prahlada in the Bhagavata Purana?
questions.explanation: Prahlada was the son of the asura king Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: To which deity was Prahlada an unwavering devotee?
questions.explanation: Prahlada is celebrated as a supreme devotee of Vishnu.

questions.q: In which Purana is the famous story of Prahlada most fully narrated?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana gives the fullest account of Prahlada's devotion.

questions.q: To which class of beings was Prahlada born?
questions.explanation: Prahlada was born among the asuras, as the son of the Daitya king Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu appeared to slay Prahlada's father?
questions.explanation: The Narasimha (man-lion) avatar appeared to slay Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Prahlada is upheld in the tradition as the supreme exemplar of which devotional path?
questions.explanation: Prahlada is regarded as the supreme exemplar of bhakti, loving devotion.

questions.q: How did Prahlada's father react to his son's devotion to Vishnu?
options: He took up the same worship
questions.explanation: Hiranyakashipu persecuted Prahlada repeatedly for worshipping his enemy Vishnu.

questions.q: After Hiranyakashipu was slain, what became of the devotee Prahlada?
options: He was spared, blessed, and made king
questions.explanation: Narasimha spared and blessed Prahlada, who then ruled the asuras justly.

questions.q: Which sacred name did the young Prahlada constantly chant despite his father's anger?
questions.explanation: Prahlada ceaselessly chanted the name of Vishnu, as Narayana or Hari.

questions.q: Prahlada's story is most closely associated with which Hindu festival?
questions.explanation: The bonfire of Holika, who tried to burn Prahlada, is commemorated at Holi.

questions.q: What was the name of Prahlada's aunt who tried to burn him in a fire?
questions.explanation: Holika, sister of Hiranyakashipu, attempted to burn Prahlada but perished herself.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, while still in the womb Prahlada received instruction in devotion from which sage?
questions.explanation: The sage Narada instructed Prahlada's mother, and so the unborn child absorbed devotion to Vishnu.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Prahlada in the Puranic accounts?
questions.explanation: Prahlada's mother was Kayadhu, the queen of Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: What were the names of the two teachers, sons of Shukra, appointed to school the young Prahlada?
questions.explanation: Shanda and Amarka, sons of the asura preceptor Shukracharya, were Prahlada's teachers.

questions.q: When Hiranyakashipu demanded to know where Vishnu was, what did Prahlada famously reply?
options: That Vishnu dwelt only in Vaikuntha
That Vishnu lived only in temples
questions.explanation: Prahlada declared that Vishnu is all-pervading and present everywhere, even in a pillar.

questions.q: From what did the Narasimha avatar burst forth when Hiranyakashipu struck it, according to the Bhagavata Purana?
questions.explanation: Narasimha emerged from a pillar that Hiranyakashipu shattered in fury.

questions.q: After Narasimha slew his father, what did the boy Prahlada do for the wrathful deity?
options: He offered prayers and pacified him
questions.explanation: Prahlada calmed the fierce Narasimha with humble prayers and praise.

questions.q: In the Puranas, Prahlada belongs to which broader lineage of demons descended from the sage Kashyapa?
questions.explanation: Prahlada was a Daitya, of the line of Diti and the sage Kashyapa.

questions.q: Which famous asura king of the Vamana avatar story is described in the Puranas as a grandson of Prahlada?
questions.explanation: The generous king Bali, of the Vamana episode, is the grandson of Prahlada.

questions.q: How is Prahlada chiefly remembered in the tradition, despite being born among the asuras?
options: As a model of pure devotion
questions.explanation: Despite his demonic birth, Prahlada is honoured as a model of pure, selfless devotion.

questions.q: When his father had Prahlada thrown from a height and into the sea, what protected the boy in the Bhagavata account?
questions.explanation: Through every torment Prahlada remained unharmed by the grace and protection of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which boon had made Hiranyakashipu believe his son's chosen god could never harm him?
options: A boon from Brahma with many conditions on his death
questions.explanation: Brahma's boon set such conditions on his death that Hiranyakashipu thought himself unkillable, a confidence Narasimha overturned.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, when asked the best of all learning, what did Prahlada teach his fellow students?
questions.explanation: Prahlada taught his classmates that devotional service to Vishnu is the highest of all learning.

questions.q: The Bhagavata Purana lists nine processes of devotion that Prahlada is said to have practised, beginning with which two?
questions.explanation: Prahlada's nine forms of devotion begin with shravana (hearing) and kirtana (chanting) of Vishnu's glories.

questions.q: According to the Vishnu Purana, what was the name of a notable son of Prahlada?
questions.explanation: Virochana, father of the famous king Bali, is named as a son of Prahlada.

questions.q: In several Puranic accounts, over which realm was the devotee Prahlada later installed as king or lord?
options: Patala or the realm of the Daityas
questions.explanation: Prahlada was made lord of the Daityas, associated with the nether realms such as Patala.

questions.q: Which great devotee of a later age is traditionally counted alongside Prahlada in lists of supreme bhaktas?
questions.explanation: Dhruva, like Prahlada, is celebrated in the Bhagavata Purana as a supreme child-devotee of Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, Narasimha placed Hiranyakashipu where to circumvent the boon, an act witnessed by Prahlada?
options: Upon his own lap at a threshold at twilight
questions.explanation: Narasimha slew the king on his lap, at a doorway threshold, at dusk, neither inside nor outside, day nor night.

questions.q: When Narasimha offered Prahlada any boon he desired, what did Prahlada chiefly ask for?
options: Freedom from material desires and steadfast devotion
questions.explanation: Prahlada asked not for worldly gain but to be free of selfish desire and fixed in devotion to Vishnu.

questions.q: The Bhagavad Gita echoes Prahlada's stature when Krishna says that among the Daityas (asuras) he is which one?
questions.explanation: In the Bhagavad Gita's tenth chapter, Krishna declares, 'Among the Daityas I am Prahlada.'
